title: Where All the Region's Rain Remembers

features.name: the confluence-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where six culverts meet. The water from different watersheds braids together and holds the memory of the land each one crossed. You are swimming through the region's history.

features.name: the rain-recording stone
features.desc-on-touch: Sediment layered on a central rock. Each band is a season, each grain a storm. The archive reads the stone like a phonograph needle reading grooves of water.

features.name: the forecast-debris
features.desc-on-touch: Objects the rainfall has carried to this place: a button, a bottle cap, a child's drawing sealed in plastic. Each one arrived on rain from a different year and the water remembers which year.

features.name: the echo-map
features.desc-on-touch: On the walls, water has traced a map of tomorrow's paddocks. The rain knows where it will fall before it stops falling. The archive compares this map to its soil records and finds them contradicting.

inscription: the water here is not flowing, it is remembering

file.name: Culvert_Confluence_Runoff_1940s2025.txt
file.note: Flood-stage records and objects found. Entries begin factual and become first-person. Final entry: 'The water spoke in my language. It told me it had been waiting.'

exits.label: back up toward the smaller culverts
following the flow toward the river-mouth
downstream into the tidal reach
exits.to: NEW: where the freshwater refuses to salt, where two waters argue in the archive's ears
